name: NeuralProphet (Neural Prophet Time Series)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Oskar Triebe / Stanford / Meta

performance.notes: PyTorch training; accelerator support; faster than Prophet for large datasets

agent_readiness.notes: Neural Prophet: hybrid of Prophet and AR-Net for time series. from neuralprophet import NeuralProphet. m = NeuralProphet(). df = pd.DataFrame({'ds': dates, 'y': values}). m.fit(df). future = m.make_future_dataframe(df, periods=30). forecast = m.predict(future). Components: trend, seasonality, AR-autoregression, lagged covariates, future regressors. m = NeuralProphet(n_lags=12, n_forecasts=6). m.add_country_holidays('US'). m.add_lagged_regressor('regressor_col'). forecast_plot = m.plot(forecast). components_plot = m.plot_components(forecast). Multiplicative seasonality. Multiple time series with global model.

gotchas: n_lags and n_forecasts must be consistent — affects model architecture
Training requires more data than Prophet due to neural component
Interpretability lower than pure Prophet with autoregression enabled
Slower to fit than Prophet on small datasets
best_when: Time series forecasting with autoregressive dependencies (stock prices, traffic)
When Prophet-style decomposition + neural AR is needed
When regressors and lagged covariates improve accuracy
avoid_when: Simple seasonal trend decomposition (use Prophet directly)
When full interpretability is required without neural AR
summary: NeuralProphet extends Facebook Prophet with neural autoregression. NeuralProphet(n_lags=12).fit(df). make_future_dataframe().predict(). Trend + seasonality + AR-Net. add_lagged_regressor(). add_country_holidays(). Multi-step forecast with n_forecasts. PyTorch backend with GPU support. MIT by Oskar Triebe/Stanford. Best for AR-dependent time series beyond Prophet's capabilities. Needs more data than Prophet. Pre-1.0 API instability.
